There are two types of ways to see yourself in your own mind's eyes. I close my eyes, alright, and I see myself in the picture in five years from now in this beautiful office with a gigantic company, all these people running around, everybody living an empowered life, all my staff that started me to help me make this a reality. They're all rich as sh**. They're all f*****g loving it. They have families, they're married, they have kids, they're in the best schools, they're all driving the cars they want, beautiful homes, everyone's living the exact life they want. In my mind, when I see myself in the picture, that's called visualizing yourself in a disassociated state, dissociated state. So watch. That's a dissociated state. Dissolving state, meaning I see myself almost from above, looking down in the picture. That's dissociated. Dr. Banley calls that going meta, like you're meta, you're above looking down, right? Versus now watch this. In five years from now, I actually step into my own body and I'm looking out through my own eyes and I'm seeing my company operating and I'm watching all my employees who I had started with me. They're running around doing their thing. They have big divisions, they're living great lives. I see their families. I see everything. But I'm seeing it through my own eyes as if I'm actually, I've stepped inside my body and I'm now feeling the feelings of success, letting them wash over, I'm feeling the greatness of what I did. I let myself feel it. That's called being in an associated state. An associated state. Guys, I am an expert at disassociated motivation. I am really, really good at seeing myself in the picture, moving in the future, doing great. I've always used that as a way to make my goals real to myself, to see myself succeeding, to future-paste my actions, to imagine myself in five years from now, and to use what I see to help guide my decisions and to help keep me motivated, in order to really motivate yourself, the power is in dissociated visualization. To see yourself like having achieved a goal, to look down with your own two-wives, and to watch Jordan Belfort or wherever you are, to watch yourself in the picture, moving through your own life, and just kicking ass. That's power. However, in terms of treating emotional and visceral gut reaction, you get a bigger gut reaction by being in an associated state. I've always found that to be more difficult personally. And I use associated visualization as very powerful for state management, because it let you trigger those old feelings back into the right states. But just to see those success, dissociated conditioning, and visual relation, much more powerful in my opinion. I find it easier. So I want you, when you're writing this vision statement out, I want you to close your eyes and see yourself five years from now, in the future, kicking ass, living the exact life you want, running the exact business you want, married to the right woman that you want, a man that you want, which is to everything, business, personal, charity, where you live, will call your driver, will close your way, where you vacation, how you communicate with people that you love, every aspect of it. And I want you to write it down. That's the key. I want you to write it down. Don't keep it locked inside of you. I want you to actually write it out and then skew. I gotta find myself. I didn't turn my phone off for a second. Sorry, I thought, guys. Let's see what this is. It is a f*****g cold cold bar right from Indian Mother f*****g. Stop cold calling me. I made a mistake in buying Viagra online. Now I have a problem, but I once bought Viagra online, they never left me alone ever since. And Dan's like, I never did that year show again, okay? Anyway. So, that being said, okay? Let's now get specific, right? With you. Where do you see yourself in five years from now? Now let me tell you the trick here to doing this right. The trick is, is that when most people go about doing this and they write up a vision statement, and usually I do this. The reason I want to do this in two shots where we actually do a special training, you know, a Q&A, right? And I want some of you guys to read back your vision statements to me so you can improve them further. Is the problem that I see is, there's only two. Number one, people forget to do this, you know, the ING. There goes one. This one, the garbage. Try purple, okay? They forget this ING, geron. And they forget to use the word IC, okay? Your vision statement starts like this. In five years from now, I see myself. That's why it's a vision statement, right? It's not a mission statement. It's a different thing. A vision statement. I see myself owning, doing, going, living. Bring it into the present using the geron, the ING conjugation of the verb, okay? And then write out exactly what you see, your best possible life in five years from now. And watch. Now let me get to the trick here, okay? Here it is. What happens, the reason that most people end up writing what I call an impotent vision statement, meaning it's impotent. It has no power. It's not powerful. It doesn't serve the purpose that it's supposed to serve, which is to move and to take action, to give them the power, the self-motivation to overcome the barriers and obstacles that are going to stand in their way, okay? The reason it's impotent is because when they're doing it, they don't give themselves the benefit of the doubt that between now and those five years, over those five years, you have learned what you need to learn. You know who you need to know. You've acquired the resources that you need to acquire. You think of five years from now, but you don't grow yourself over those five years. You don't include the expansion of your resources and contacts and skill sets.
